perturbation.original_text: Base Servicing Fee: As to any Mortgage Loan and any Collection Period, an amount equal to the product of the Base Servicing Fee Rate, the UPB of that Mortgage Loan as of the related Measurement Date and 1/12 or, for the first Collection Period, the number of days in such Collection Period divided by 360; provided, however, that payment of the Base Servicing Fee for any delinquent Mortgage Loan shall be suspended unless and until Freedom Mortgage recovers the amount thereof from payments in respect thereof from the related mortgagor or the amount thereof is otherwise recovered from liquidation of the related property.
perturbation.changed_text: Base Servicing Fee: As to any Mortgage Loan and any Collection Period, an amount equal to the product of the Standard Servicing Fee Rate, the UPB of that Mortgage Loan as of the related Measurement Date and 1/12 or, for the first Collection Period, the number of days in such Collection Period divided by 360; provided, however, that payment of the Base Servicing Fee for any delinquent Mortgage Loan shall be suspended unless and until Freedom Mortgage recovers the amount thereof from payments in respect thereof from the related mortgagor or the amount thereof is otherwise recovered from liquidation of the related property.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Base Servicing Fee Rate' is changed to 'Standard Servicing Fee Rate.' This change introduces uncertainty because 'Base Servicing Fee Rate' is defined later in the same section, making it unclear which rate should be used for calculation. This affects the calculation of fees due to the contradiction in the term used. This will lead to the in-text contradiction where the payment and rate are defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Base Servicing Fee: As to any Mortgage Loan and any Collection Period, an amount equal to the product of the Standard Servicing Fee Rate, the UPB of that Mortgage Loan as of the related Measurement Date and 1/12 or, for the first Collection Period, the number of days in such Collection Period divided by 360; provided, however, that payment of the Base Servicing Fee for any delinquent Mortgage Loan shall be suspended unless and until Freedom Mortgage recovers the amount thereof from payments in respect thereof from the related mortgagor or the amount thereof is otherwise recovered from liquidation of the related property.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Base Servicing Fee Rate' to 'Standard Servicing Fee Rate' introduces a contradiction because the contract defines 'Base Servicing Fee Rate' elsewhere. The contradictory text also uses 'Standard Servicing Fee Rate,' leading to confusion about which rate should be used, creating a conflict in the payment and rate definition. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition where multiple terms are used interchangeably without clear definitions, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Freedom Mortgage represents and warrants to Cherry Hill that the statements contained in this Section 4(a) are true and correct in all material respects as of the date of this Agreement (or, if made as of a different specified date, as of such date) and will be true and correct in all material respects as of the Closing Date (as though made then and as though the Closing Date were substituted for the date of this Agreement throughout this Section 4(a)).
perturbation.changed_text: Freedom Mortgage affirms and assures to Cherry Hill that the statements contained in this Section 4(a) are true and correct in all material respects as of the date of this Agreement (or, if made as of a different specified date, as of such date) and will be true and correct in all material respects as of the Closing Date (as though made then and as though the Closing Date were substituted for the date of this Agreement throughout this Section 4(a)).
perturbation.explanation: The term 'represents and warrants' is changed to 'affirms and assures.' Although seemingly synonymous, 'represents and warrants' is a standard legal phrase creating specific legal obligations and potential liabilities, while 'affirms and assures' is more ambiguous. By changing the established legal terminology, the perturbation introduces uncertainty about the strength and enforceability of Freedom Mortgage's commitments. This creates an in-text contradiction as to the legal standard applied to the statements made by Freedom Mortgage.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Freedom Mortgage affirms and assures to Cherry Hill that the statements contained in this Section 4(a) are true and correct in all material respects as of the date of this Agreement (or, if made as of a different specified date, as of such date) and will be true and correct in all material respects as of the Closing Date (as though made then and as though the Closing Date were substituted for the date of this Agreement throughout this Section 4(a)).
perturbation.justification: The change from 'represents and warrants' to 'affirms and assures' weakens the legal enforceability of Freedom Mortgage's statements. While both phrases suggest truthfulness, 'represents and warrants' is a legally established term with specific consequences for breach, while 'affirms and assures' is less defined and provides less legal recourse. Since the contradicted text is the exact same as the Changed Text, the changed text has created an internal contradiction as to the legal standard.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that a party materially breaches any representation or covenant herein, the other party may give written notice of the breach requiring the same to be remedied within 30 days of receipt of such notice. If the breaching party fails to remedy the material breach in such time period, the non-breaching party may terminate this Agreement by delivery of a written termination notice to the breaching party. Any such termination shall not relieve the breaching party from any obligation or liability arising prior to such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that a party seriously violates any representation or covenant herein, the other party may give formal notification of the violation requiring the same to be addressed within 30 days of receipt of such notice. If the violating party fails to address the serious violation in such time period, the non-violating party may end this Agreement by delivery of a written ending notification to the violating party. Any such ending shall not relieve the violating party from any obligation or liability arising prior to such ending.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'materially breaches' is changed to 'seriously violates,' 'written notice of the breach' to 'formal notification of the violation,' 'remedied' to 'addressed,' 'non-breaching party' to 'non-violating party,' 'terminate this Agreement' to 'end this Agreement,' and 'termination notice' to 'ending notification.' While aiming for simplicity, these changes introduce potential ambiguity. 'Seriously violates' lacks the legal precision of 'materially breaches,' potentially altering the threshold for triggering the termination clause. Similarly, 'addressed' is less definitive than 'remedied,' leaving room for interpretation. This affects the enforcement of the agreement because the threshold for termination is unclear. This affects the termination clause of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that a party seriously violates any representation or covenant herein, the other party may give formal notification of the violation requiring the same to be addressed within 30 days of receipt of such notice. If the violating party fails to address the serious violation in such time period, the non-violating party may end this Agreement by delivery of a written ending notification to the violating party. Any such ending shall not relieve the violating party from any obligation or liability arising prior to such ending.
perturbation.justification: The changed text and contradicted text are identical in this scenario. While the explanation suggests that the changed language introduces ambiguity by using terms like 'seriously violates' instead of 'materially breaches,' the contradiction is not strong enough to warrant a 'YES.' The standard for 'serious violation' is open to interpretation, but it doesn't create a direct, undeniable conflict within the document. The original text was modified in a way that, according to the explanation, introduces potential ambiguity, but this ambiguity doesn't necessarily create a strong and direct conflict. Therefore, it defaults to 'NO'.
